steps.title: Identify the compensation mechanism
steps.content: Besterman and Pace found benthic chlorophyll did not decrease with macroalgal proliferation—contradicting the theory's prediction. But they noted that MPB respond to low-light conditions by producing higher-than-normal chlorophyll. This compensation mechanism explains their unexpected result.

steps.title: Connect to the conclusion
steps.content: Choice A directly states the implication: because of compensation capacity, benthic chlorophyll may not reliably indicate MPB biomass—the proxy becomes unreliable when elevated chlorophyll masks declining biomass. Choices B, C, and D each fail: B denies compensation is relevant, C invokes an unmentioned mechanism, D misattributes the discrepancy to light consistency. That matches choice A — A is correct.
